selected_pmids.reason: This recent study investigates how transcranial magneto-acoustic electrical stimulation affects dendritic spines and synaptic plasticity specifically in hippocampal CA1 pyramidal neurons in an Alzheimer's disease mouse model, aligning closely with the topic of CA1 pyramidal neuron dendritic spines.

This research analyzes autophagy's impact on dendritic spine morphology across proximal and distal dendritic compartments of hippocampal CA1 pyramidal neurons, providing detailed insight into dendritic spine regulation.
This paper examines dendritic arborization and spine density alterations in CA1 pyramidal neurons, offering relevant data on dendritic structure changes important for understanding spine morphology.
The study focuses on spatial distribution of potentiated dendritic spines in CA1 pyramidal neurons during memory encoding and recall, directly addressing functional aspects of dendritic spines.

This study evaluates the effect of canagliflozin on dendritic spine density and dendrite morphology in CA1 pyramidal neurons within an Alzheimer's disease model, involving experimental spine density and morphology analysis.
